axis_responsibility: Brick owns the attack-QA work contract and required return shape; this is the chosen repair surface.
Agent performs the attack QA and returns observed facts in AgentFact.returned; it does not classify success, failure, approval, or quality.
Link carries returned public facts and owns Movement/gate sufficiency; the new fields do not choose route or Movement.
Support checker verifies template completeness and prose/YAML sync only; checker green would be support evidence, not source truth or quality judgment.
candidate_file_changes: brick/templates/bricks/code-attack-qa/brick.md: add outer-lens duty paragraph and add symptom_reproduction, own_probes, real_entry_surface to Return prose.
brick/templates/bricks/code-attack-qa/return.yaml: add symptom_reproduction, own_probes, real_entry_surface to required_return_shape.
brick/templates/bricks/axis-attack-qa/brick.md: add outer-lens duty paragraph and add symptom_reproduction, own_probes, real_entry_surface to Return prose.
brick/templates/bricks/axis-attack-qa/return.yaml: add symptom_reproduction, own_probes, real_entry_surface to required_return_shape.
support/checkers/check_bricks_spec_completeness.py: no candidate change unless a future field-set pin exists; current prose/YAML drift coverage already covers the proposed field additions.
checker_or_verifier_plan: After edits, run direct focused checker: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 PYTHONPATH=support/import_identity python3 support/checkers/check_bricks_spec_completeness.py.
Run required focused profile: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 PYTHONPATH=support/import_identity python3 support/checkers/check_profile.py --profile core.
Run full gate from a temp source copy after baking, then report the temp copy path and --all exit code.
Because this environment is read-only and lacks a writable temporary directory, current checker green and temp-copy full gate are not proven here.
returned.design_summary: Use new top-level required return fields, not only nested observed_evidence items. The outer-lens triple is a required evidence contract for every code-attack-qa and axis-attack-qa performance, and top-level required_return_shape entries give the Link gate a stable presence check while still mapping only to AgentFact.returned. The fields are factual evidence labels, not status, verdict, quality, route, or Movement authority.
edge_cases: Task source has no concrete reported symptom: symptom_reproduction records that no reproducible symptom was present and not_proven names symptom absence; this avoids inventing a symptom.
Only builder fixtures exist: QA may inspect them as evidence but must create or select separate probes; otherwise own_probes records the gap and not_proven names builder-fixture-only coverage.
Real entry surface is destructive or requires credentials: do not drive it; real_entry_surface records the blocked surface and not_proven names the safety or credential limit.

Axis attack QA may inspect documentation/projection authority rather than executable code: real_entry_surface can be the actual checker/profile/projection/read verb a caller would use, not an internal helper fixture.
A new field in return.yaml without exact prose naming will fail the existing prose_return_shape_drift check.
invariants: Every attack-QA return names symptom_reproduction, own_probes, and real_entry_surface.
Builder or upstream fixtures cannot satisfy own_probes.
At least one real entry surface must be driven when a safe/read-only real entry surface exists.
If reproduction or a real entry surface cannot be driven, the required field still records the attempted path and not_proven names the missing evidence.
evidence_used must continue to cite inspected repository artifacts, not packet labels only.
No mutation, no verdict keys, no Movement choice, no route target, no credential/session body, no scheduler, no new dependency.
not_proven: No files were edited in this read-only run.
Focused checker green is not proven because the checker could not allocate a temporary directory in this environment.
Full gate --all in a temp source copy is not proven because the task forbids writes here and no writable temp directory was available.
Future QA performance quality is not proven.
Source truth, success judgment, quality judgment, and Movement authority are not claimed.
No Claude/Gemini/model review evidence was collected in this run.
observed_evidence: Read brick/templates/bricks/code-attack-qa/brick.md:16-30: current code attack QA is read-only, attacks declared work contract, requires inspected repository artifact refs in evidence_used, but does not currently require independent symptom reproduction, QA-owned probes distinct from builder fixtures, or a real caller entry surface.

Read brick/templates/bricks/code-attack-qa/brick.md:32-47 and return.yaml:7-17: current required return shape lacks symptom_reproduction, own_probes, and real_entry_surface while preserving forbidden verdict and Movement keys.

Read brick/templates/bricks/axis-attack-qa/brick.md:16-27: current axis attack QA is read-only and inspects Brick/Agent/Link ownership, support leakage, projection authority, and evidence integrity, but does not currently require outer-lens reproduction.

Read brick/templates/bricks/axis-attack-qa/brick.md:29-41 and return.yaml:7-17: current required return shape lacks the outer-lens evidence triple while preserving non-binding transition concern evidence and forbidden verdict/Movement keys.

Read support/checkers/check_bricks_spec_completeness.py:33-42,137-142,158-198,210-219,441-473: the checker requires frontmatter completeness, non-empty body, resolvable primary return templates, and every primary return.yaml required field named in brick.md body prose.

Attempted PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 PYTHONPATH=support/import_identity python3 support/checkers/check_bricks_spec_completeness.py; it did not reach contract validation because the read-only environment had no usable writable temporary directory.
returned.output_excerpt: {"observed_evidence":["Read brick/templates/bricks/code-attack-qa/brick.md:16-30: current code attack QA is read-only, attacks declared work contract, requires inspected repository artifact refs in evidence_used, but does not currently require independent symptom reproduction, QA-owned probes distinct from builder fixtures, or a real caller entry surface.","Read brick/templates/bricks/code-attack-qa/brick.md:32-47 and return.yaml:7-17: current required return shape lacks symptom_reproduction, own_probes, and real_entry_surface while preserving forbidden verdict and Movement keys.","Read bri...
proposed_changes: Add required_return_shape entries symptom_reproduction, own_probes, and real_entry_surface to both return.yaml files, placed after attacked_work or attacked_scope so the outer-lens facts are adjacent to the attacked target.

In both brick.md bodies, add an Outer-lens duty paragraph requiring QA to independently reproduce the reported symptom from the task source, build QA-owned probes distinct from upstream or builder fixtures, and drive at least one real entry surface used by a real caller.
In both Return sections, add the exact field names symptom_reproduction, own_probes, and real_entry_surface to keep PROSE<->YAML sync.

Define symptom_reproduction as what symptom from the task source was independently reproduced, how it was driven, and what output was observed; if the task source contains no reproducible symptom, the field must say that and not_proven must name the missing fact.
Define own_probes as probes built or selected by QA for this attack, with upstream or builder fixtures allowed only as inspected evidence, not counted as QA-owned probes.

Define real_entry_surface as the real verb, projection, CLI, checker profile, import path, or caller-facing surface driven, plus observed output/ref; helper-only or fixture-only calls do not satisfy it.
relevant_current_structure: code-attack-qa currently returns observed_evidence, attacked_work, checked_sources, regression_risks, negative_probe_observations, failing_or_missing_probes, boundary_violations, transition_concern_evidence, evidence_used, not_proven.

axis-attack-qa currently returns observed_evidence, attacked_scope, brick_axis_findings, agent_axis_findings, link_axis_findings, support_leak_findings, projection_authority_findings, transition_concern_evidence, evidence_used, not_proven.
Both templates already forbid success, failure, approved, quality, movement_choice, and route_target.
Both brick.md files already require inspected repository artifact references in evidence_used.
The checker will reject a new return.yaml field unless the exact field name appears in the corresponding brick.md body prose.
returned.returned_summary: local CLI Agent Adapter returned support evidence
unchanged_surfaces: No change to requires_brick_write_scope: both QA bricks remain read-only.
No change to link_movement_literal: both remain forward unless a declared Link gate later acts.
No change to transition_concern_evidence authority: it remains non-binding evidence only.
No change to forbidden_return_keys in either return.yaml.
No change to agent resources, Link resources, support/operator, support/connection, project status, AGENTS.md, pyproject.toml, uv.lock, or git state.
